title: Lesson 04: Asking Basic Questions
description: Learn the essential interrogative words (Who, What, Where) and how to form questions naturally.

content.heading: The Question Words
lines: In Pashto, question words typically sit right before the verb.

content.heading: Forming Questions
lines: What is this?: Da tse day? [Dah tsay day?] (Literally: This what is?)
Who is this?: Da tsok day? [Dah tsoak day?] (Literally: This who is?)
Where are you?: Te chera ye? [Tuh cheh-rah yay?] (Literally: You where are?)

content.text: Cultural Pro-Tip: In Kabul, 'Da tse day?' is incredibly useful at the bazaar when pointing at fruits or items you don't know the name of yet. Merchants love it when foreigners try to learn item names!

content.question: How do you ask 'Where are you?' in Pashto?
